message:
feat(net): implement RFC 8305 Happy Eyeballs v2 concurrent connection racing

Replace the sequential fallback-based Happy Eyeballs implementation in the
DNS resolver with a proper concurrent dual-stack connection racing algorithm
per RFC 8305. The new implementation staggers connection attempts with
configurable delays, races IPv6 and IPv4 concurrently, and returns the first
successful connection.

Key changes:
- Add src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs with HappyEyeballsConfig, sort_addresses()
  for IPv6-first interleaved ordering, and connect() using a custom
  RaceConnections future that polls multiple TCP connections in parallel
- Wire up module export and re-export HappyEyeballsConfig + connect from
  src/net/mod.rs
- Refactor dns/resolver.rs connect_happy_eyeballs() to delegate to the new
  concurrent implementation instead of sleeping between sequential attempts
- Comprehensive test suite covering address interleaving, config defaults,
  edge cases (empty addrs, zero timeout), and stagger schedule verification

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
